items.title: Investors Have to Be Ready for State Capitalism. What Areas to Watch.
items.description: Jefferies has a list of companies and industries that could see investment interest from the federal government.

items.title: BAESY Wins $1.7B Deal for Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System
items.description: BAE Systems secures a major $1.74 billion APKWS contract, extending its role in delivering combat-proven precision rocket systems.

items.title: Boeing (BA) Expands 737 MAX Orders with Macquarie AirFinance Commitment
items.description: Boeing (BA) recently announced a partnership with Macquarie AirFinance for 30 additional 737-8 jets, highlighting its expanding commitment to airline fleet modernization and sustainability. Over the last quarter, Boeing’s stock rose 11%, which contrasts against the broader market's volatility, further influenced by mixed economic signals and a steep decline in the tech sector. Boeing’s new orders from major airlines like Korean Air and Cathay Pacific, alongside the positive earnings outcomes...

items.title: Ryanair Issues Impressive Traffic Numbers for August 2025
items.description: RYAAY reports an August 2025 load factor of 96%, which remained flat on a year-over-year basis and sequentially.

items.title: Air Lease Is Going Private. Why Boeing Stock Is Up.
items.description: A big aircraft lessor is going private, a modest signal of confidence in the outlook for the aviation industry.  Tuesday,  Air Lease  said it had agreed to be acquired by investors including Sumitomo, SMBC Aviation Capital, investment vehicles affiliated with  Apollo  and  Brookfield  Shareholders are getting $65 per share in cash.  Air Lease stock was up 6.6% in midday trading, at $64.17, while the  and  were down 1.4% and 1.1%, respectively.
